key_points: This narrative review evaluates Er:YAG laser-activated irrigation (LAI) and photon-induced photoacoustic streaming (PIPS) as endodontic irrigation activation methods, based on 59 selected articles from PubMed.
PIPS uses sub-ablative energy settings (20 mJ, 15 Hz) with ultra-short pulses (50 µs) to generate intracanal cavitation and shockwaves via photoacoustic and photomechanical effects.
Er:YAG LAI and PIPS outperformed other irrigation activation methods in 33 of the 59 reviewed articles.
No in vivo studies were found; all evidence is based on in vitro or ex vivo study designs.

Evidence suggests Er:YAG LAI and PIPS are promising for canal disinfection and debris/smear layer removal, but large variability in study designs (bacterial incubation time, laser parameters, irrigation protocols, irrigating solutions) limits the strength of conclusions.
Further in vivo studies are needed to better evaluate the clinical efficiency of these techniques.

outcomes.result: Er:YAG LAI and PIPS outperformed other methods in 33/59 articles
outcomes.clinical_significance: Promising results for canal disinfection but limited by high variability in study designs and absence of in vivo evidence

outcomes.result: Er:YAG LAI and PIPS showed superior performance compared to other irrigation methods in multiple studies
outcomes.clinical_significance: Potentially clinically relevant but requires confirmation in in vivo settings

notes: Review included 59 articles selected from PubMed. No in vivo studies were identified. High heterogeneity in study designs limits generalizability of findings.

population.sample_type: In vitro / ex vivo studies (no in vivo studies found)

superiority_claim: El autor afirma que 'Er:YAG LAI and PIPS outperformed other methods in 33 of the 59 articles' y los describe como 'a revolutionary and powerful method to activate the irrigant', aunque reconoce que la variabilidad metodológica limita la significancia de los resultados.
source_citation: Linh, Q., & Gaudin, A. (2020). The Efficiency of the Er:YAG Laser and Photon-Induced Photoacoustic Streaming (PIPS) as an Activation Method in Endodontic Irrigation: A Literature Review. Journal of Lasers in Medical Sciences, 11(3). https://doi.org/10.34172/jlms.2020.53

treatment_protocol.application_technique: intracanal photoacoustic streaming (PIPS tip positioned in coronal third)

clinical_indication: Endodontic irrigation activation for canal disinfection, debris removal, and smear layer removal
uncertainties: The PIPS parameters (20 mJ, 15 Hz, 50 µs) are cited as representative/typical values from the literature; individual studies within the review may have used different parameters.
No conflict of interest or funding information was available in the abstract.
Full text was not available; some details about specific study designs and additional laser parameters from included studies could not be extracted.
The review does not provide a systematic quality assessment of included studies, limiting evidence level assignment.
